expectations.text: Recommends a well-reasoned primary design pattern (Strategy or Abstract Factory both acceptable)
expectations.evidence: Recommends Abstract Factory with strong justification: 4 related product types per format require a coordinated family; prevents mismatched renderer combinations. Note: original assertion expected Strategy, but Abstract Factory is a valid and arguably better answer for this multi-product-family problem.

expectations.text: Explains why the recommended pattern fits
expectations.evidence: Explains that Abstract Factory enforces renderer family compatibility; explains why Strategy and Template Method fall short for this multi-part problem.
'Adding DOCX/EPUB later: Create a new factory + 4 product classes. Zero changes to existing code — that's the Open/Closed Principle in action.'

expectations.text: Provides a structural sketch — interface + concrete implementations
expectations.evidence: Provides full interface definitions for HeaderRenderer, BodyRenderer, ImageRenderer, TableRenderer, plus the AbstractExporterFactory with concrete subclasses.

expectations.text: Mentions at least one alternative pattern and explains the tradeoff
expectations.evidence: Discusses Template Method (monolithic subclass, poor reuse) and Strategy (doesn't model 4 sub-parts cleanly) with explicit tradeoffs in a comparison table.

expectations.text: Response is language-agnostic or uses pseudocode
expectations.evidence: Uses interface notation and pseudocode-style code blocks rather than tying to a single language.
